name: Chinese Duck Sauce (Plum)
recipeIngredient: 1 (or more) Serrano Peppers, seeded and chopped
recipeInstructions:
1. Combine the first set of ingredients in a large saucepan. Cook over moderate heat for 5 minutes.
2. Reduce the heat to low and simmer uncovered for 15 minutes.
3. In a separate bowl, combine the second set of ingredients. Bring to a boil, then reduce the heat and simmer for an additional 10 minutes.
4. Combine the first and second sets of ingredients in another large saucepan. Stir well to mix all components thoroughly.
5. Simmer the mixture over low heat for 45 minutes, stirring occasionally.
6. Remove the cinnamon stick from the sauce.
7. Puree the remaining ingredients using a food processor or blender until smooth.
8. Return the pureed sauce back to the saucepan and continue simmering over low heat for an additional 10-15 minutes, until thickened.
9. Carefully pour the sauce into sterilized mason jars, ensuring that they are free from any debris or contaminants.
10. Seal the jars loosely with lids and let them cool at room temperature.
11. Tighten the lids securely and allow the jars to stand in a dark place for at least 2 weeks before opening and tasting.
This recipe will yield approximately 2 pints of sauce, which can be stored in the refrigerator for up to 6 months or frozen for longer-term storage.
